---
format: 
  pdf: 
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    keep-tex: false
    documentclass: article
    geometry:
      - top=10mm
      - left=15mm
      - right=15mm
      - bottom=5mm
      - heightrounded
    fontfamily: libertine
    colorlinks: true
    fontsize: '18'
    include-in-header: 
      text: |
        \usepackage{multicol}
        \usepackage{lipsum}
        \usepackage{hyperref}
        \usepackage{libertine}
        \usepackage{fancyhdr}
        \usepackage{lastpage}
        \setlength{\multicolsep}{2pt plus 1.0pt minus 0.75pt}
        \setlength{\columnsep}{3em}
        \usepackage{enumitem}
        \setlist{nolistsep}
        \fancyhf{}
        \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0pt}
        \fancyfoot[C]{\thepage/\pageref{LastPage}}
        \AtBeginDocument{\pagestyle{fancy}}
---

<!-- TITLE -->
\begin{huge}\begin{center}\textbf{SIMONE ROMITI}\end{center}\end{huge}

<!-- CONTACT INFO -->
\begin{center}
\href{mailto:simone.romiti.1994@gmail.com}{simone.romiti.1994@gmail.com} |
\href{https://simone-romiti.github.io/}{Website} | 
\href{https://github.com/simone-romiti}{GitHub} | 
\href{https://www.linkedin.com/in/simone-romiti/}{LinkedIn} |
\href{https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6509-447X}{ORCID}
\end{center}
\vspace{15pt}

\hrule

\begin{normalsize}

Computational physicist with a PhD and 4+ years of postdoctoral research experience in Monte Carlo simulations, Bayesian inference, and high-performance computing. Production-grade C++ and Python developer; main author and maintainer of open-source scientific libraries with CI/CD and versioned releases. 
Comfortable owning numerical methods end-to-end: derivation, implementation, statistical validation, and uncertainty quantification.

Resident in Switzerland with a B permit.

\end{normalsize}

<!-- EDUCATION -->
\begin{normalsize}\textbf{EDUCATION}
  \vspace{5pt}
  \hrule
  \vspace{5pt}

  \begin{multicols}{2}
    \begin{flushleft}
    \textbf{PhD in Physics} | title awarded in April 2022
    \end{flushleft}
    \begin{flushright}\href{https://www.uniroma3.it/}{\textit{Roma Tre University}} | Nov. 2018 -- Oct. 2021\end{flushright}
  \end{multicols}
  \vspace{-0.2cm}

  \begin{multicols}{2}
    \begin{flushleft}
    \textbf{M.S.\ in Physics} | Grade: 110/110 \textit{cum laude} | GPA: 3.98 / 4
    \end{flushleft}
    \begin{flushright}\href{https://www.uniroma3.it/}{\textit{Roma Tre University}} | Oct. 2016 -- Oct. 2018\end{flushright}
  \end{multicols}

  \begin{multicols}{2}
    \begin{flushleft}
    \textbf{B.S.\ in Physics} | Grade: 110/110 \textit{cum laude} | GPA: 3.85 / 4
    \end{flushleft}
    \begin{flushright}\href{https://www.uniroma3.it/}{\textit{Roma Tre University}} | Oct. 2013 -- Jul. 2016\end{flushright}
  \end{multicols}

\end{normalsize}

<!-- WORK EXPERIENCE -->
\begin{normalsize}\textbf{WORK EXPERIENCE}
  \vspace{5pt}
  \hrule
  \vspace{5pt}

  \begin{multicols}{2}
    \begin{flushleft}\textbf{\href{https://www.unibe.ch/index_eng.html}{University of Bern}}\end{flushleft}
    \begin{flushright}Apr 2024 -- Present\end{flushright}
  \end{multicols}
  \vspace{-0.15cm}
  \begin{multicols}{2}
    \begin{flushleft}\textit{Postdoctoral Researcher} | Software developer for scientific applications \end{flushleft}
    \begin{flushright}Bern, Switzerland\end{flushright}
  \end{multicols}
\end{normalsize}

\begin{normalsize}
  \begin{multicols}{2}
    \begin{flushleft}\textbf{\href{https://www.uni-bonn.de/en/home?set_language=en}{University of Bonn}}\end{flushleft}
    \begin{flushright}Nov 2021 -- Mar 2024\end{flushright}
  \end{multicols}
  \vspace{-0.15cm}
  \begin{multicols}{2}
    \begin{flushleft}\textit{Postdoctoral Researcher} | Software developer for scientific applications \end{flushleft}
    \begin{flushright}Bonn, Germany\end{flushright}
  \end{multicols}
\end{normalsize}

<!-- COMPETENCES -->
\begin{normalsize}\textbf{COMPETENCES}

\vspace{8pt}
\hrule

\begin{itemize}
\item
\textbf{Design and implementation of algorithms} for high-dimensional integrals with Markov Chain Monte Carlo, Langevin dynamics, Nested Sampling. Spectral methods for time series analysis.
\item
\textbf{Machine Learning applications to the solution of Partial Differential Equations} with Physics-Informed Neural Networks, convolutional networks, and diffusion models.
\item
\textbf{Statistical analysis of terabyte-scale datasets} from data production to inference. Autocorrelation analysis, Akaike model averaging, and error budgeting.
\item
\textbf{Code optimization via parallel computing} using CPU and GPU offloading. Scaling-based optimizations for memory consumption and computational cost.
\item
\textbf{Scientific writing and reporting}: author of peer-reviewed papers in scientific journals, invited speaker at conferences and workshops, and organizer of scientific events.
\item
\textbf{Mentoring} and technical support for Master's and PhD students.
\end{itemize}

\end{normalsize}

<!-- SKILLS -->
\begin{normalsize}\textbf{SOFTWARE TOOLS}
  \vspace{5pt}
  \hrule
  \vspace{5pt}

\textbf{Languages} - Bash, C/C++, Python, R

\vspace{-0.15cm}
\textbf{Python libraries} - PyTorch, NumPy, SciPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, Plotly, Streamlit, SymPy, Jupyter

\vspace{-0.15cm}
\textbf{HPC \& Systems} - CUDA, MPI, OpenMP, SLURM, EasyBuild

\vspace{-0.15cm}
\textbf{DevOps \& Tools} - Git, GitHub Actions, CI/CD, Docker, LaTeX, Quarto

\vspace{-0.15cm}
\textbf{Spoken Languages} - Italian (native), English (proficient), German (basic)

\end{normalsize}

<!-- ACHIEVEMENTS -->
\begin{normalsize}\textbf{ACHIEVEMENTS}

\vspace{8pt}
\hrule

\begin{itemize}
    \item Developed novel algorithm using \textbf{Physics-Informed Neural Network} for solving the Schrödinger equation.
    \item \textbf{Principal Investigator} for $O(10^6)$ GPU-hour allocations on CSCS ALPS and GCS JUPITER 2026.
    \item Designed and operated \textbf{large-scale Monte Carlo simulations} on international HPC clusters, improving sampling efficiency.
    \item \textbf{Reduction of algorithmic complexity} for a class of multi-dimensional integrals from exponential to $O(N \log N)$ using parallelized Fast Fourier Transforms.
    \item Derived and implemented a \textbf{numerical method achieving machine-precision accuracy} for a class of constrained optimization problems.
    \item Developed a \textbf{Hybrid Monte Carlo + quantum-computing} method for estimating high-dimensional integrals.
  \end{itemize}
\end{normalsize}

\vspace{8pt}

<!-- OPEN-SOURCE PROJECTS -->
\begin{normalsize}\textbf{SELECTED OPEN-SOURCE PROJECTS}
  \vspace{5pt}
  \hrule
  \vspace{5pt}
\end{normalsize}
\vspace{-0.15cm}

\begin{normalsize}
  \begin{itemize}
    \item \href{https://github.com/simone-romiti/lattice_data_tools}{\textbf{lattice\_data\_tools}} -- Python toolkit for the production and statistical analysis of lattice simulation data. Main author and maintainer.
    \item \href{https://github.com/HISKP-LQCD/su2/tree/nested_sampling}{\textbf{su2}} -- C++ templated library for Monte Carlo simulation of SU(N) theories with OpenMP parallelization. Main editor.
    \item \href{https://github.com/simone-romiti/adiabatic_PINNs-suN}{\textbf{adiabatic\_PINNs-suN}} -- Physics-Informed Neural Network solver for the Schrödinger equation in SU(N) lattice gauge theory; reference implementation for sole-author publication.
    \item \href{https://github.com/etmc/tmLQCD}{\textbf{tmLQCD}} -- Large-scale C library for lattice simulations on GPU clusters (CUDA + MPI). Contributor.
  \end{itemize}
\end{normalsize}
